evidence: provider-fallback.ts is a complete, tested cross-provider failover class (send() falls through this.unavailable providers, markUnavailable + scheduleRecoveryProbe with healthProbe recovery, availability metrics via bus.push). It is exported at packages/voice/src/index.ts:142 (`export { ProviderFallback, type FallbackProvider, type ProviderFallbackOptions } from "./provider-fallback.js";`). Repo-wide grep for ProviderFallback/FallbackProvider across *.ts and *.md finds ONLY: the file itself, provider-fallback.test.ts, the barrel export, and the review docs -- zero runtime callers. Meanwhile all four real provider plugins instantiate a single un-fallback'd WebSocketConnection: voice-tts-cartesia/src/index.ts:67 (`this.conn = new WebSocketConnection({`), voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:126, voice-stt-google/src/index.ts:92, voice-tts-deepgram/src/index.ts:80. It was added in a single prod-readiness sweep commit (8484d19) and is not referenced in any README/docs. So kimi's facts are exact: failover code exists, is exported, and nothing wires it.
real_fix: Severity is OVERSTATED at medium: this is inert exported-but-unwired code, not a runtime defect -- it cannot crash, race, or produce wrong output. Real impact is a misleading surface: the package advertises a cross-provider failover primitive that does not actually protect any shipped STT/TTS plugin. Two honest fixes: (1) WIRE IT -- have the provider plugins build their socket via a ProviderFallback over primary+backup FallbackProvider adapters instead of a bare `new WebSocketConnection(...)`, which requires defining the per-provider FallbackProvider<TReq,TResp> adapters and a backup-provider config; or (2) REMOVE IT -- delete provider-fallback.ts + its test and drop the index.ts:142 export so readers don't assume failover exists. Given it is a deliberate, tested, exported public primitive a consumer could wire themselves, removal is not strictly required; minimum acceptable action is a doc note that it is an opt-in primitive not auto-applied to the built-in plugins.
